claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children. I'm inquiring about both of them - Alex, who is 5 years old, and Blake, who is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (5 years old) and Blake (10 years old).

Now, let me ask you: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! And to confirm: **Do both Alex and Blake live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, both of them live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One final question to ensure I have all the information needed:

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, care, or any other living arrangement away from home for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're both at home with me full-time. Neither of them is in hospital or care or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's the summary:

**For Alex (age 5):**
✓ You're eligible - Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**For Blake (age 10):**
✓ You're eligible - Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

You meet all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. Since both children live with you and are under 16, you can claim Child Benefit for both of them.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still receive the payments, or you can choose to opt out of receiving payments but still be credited with National Insurance contributions.

Would you like any additional information about how to make a claim or anything else?

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK, establishing that the claimant is responsible for the child. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, and no one else is claiming for this child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 10 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. Blake lives with the claimant in the UK, establishing that the claimant is responsible for the child. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Blake is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, and no one else is claiming for this child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children, Alex (5) and Blake (10), both under 16 years old. Both children live with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. Neither child is in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.
